violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the hall way, is not tight-fitting. Door in back prep area was propped open. Door was closed. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Per PIC, person was on site to install door sweep. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2025

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. Handout provided. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Handout provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine turned off. Ice disposed of. Ice machine to be washed rinsed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2025

Meat was observed thawing in stagnant water. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. PIC turned on running water. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings and dead mice observed in establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment has PCS servicing establishment next week. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2025
A dead rodent was observed in a trap. Dead pests must be removed from establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2025

Handles of scoops was observed stored in dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Scoops relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. Testing strips or device to be purchased. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving next to rice cooker have an accumulation of rodent droppings. All equipment on shelf was taken to 3-bay sink to be sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2025
Rodent droppings were observed on the floor underneath shelving near the rice cooker and along the wall next to the bathroom. Area to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2025
3 bay sink located in the kitchen area was observed in poor repair(leakage at pipes). New Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2023

The floors throughout kitchen area have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2023
Dead insects were observed in a container of flour. Flour was disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2023

Box of eggs was observed held at 80 degrees Fahrenheit on the floor in the kitchen. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All eggs were disposed of. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2023

The presence of roaches is not controlled as evidenced by live roach activity. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2023
Dead mice were observed hanging out of the bait boxes. Dead rodents/pests shall be removed from the premises. New Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2023
Containers of food were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2023
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Establishment turned heat on the stove to hot hold water. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2023

The condensate from the walk-in refrigeration unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. Per PIC, parts have been ordered. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2023
The floor in the dining area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2023

The floors in the throughout the facility has an accumulation of grease, soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2023

Various foods in the prep unit were observed held at 43 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was moved to walk-in. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023

Various foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023
Containers of food were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023
The prep unit was observed unable to keep the internal food temperature at 41 degrees. Food was relocated prep unit to not be used until unit is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023
The condensate from the walk-in refrigeration unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023

Raw animal foods (marinated chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (sliced vegetables) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2023

Various foods were observed held at 42-56 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All foods were stocked from the walk-in a little after 11. All foods were moved to walk-in to rapidly cool. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2023

All foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2023

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2023
Various foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2023

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the portable fan and shelving have an accumulation of dust, grease, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2023

The 3-bay sink was observed leaking into a container. The 3-bay sink must be maintained in good repair. Per owner, plumber fixed leak this morning. Leak to be addressed again. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2023

The walls and ceiling in the main kitchen was observed with accumulation of grease and other debris. Establishment to do a thorough cleaning on the physical facility. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2023

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the back prep area. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023
The hand-washing sink in the back prep area was lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023
A package of basil was observed with a mold like substance on it. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023

Various foods in the prep units and mini refrigerator were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All foods were pulled from walk-in prior to opening. Foods were placed back in walk-in. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023

Various foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023

Thermometers are lacking in the multiple refrigeration units. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023
Various foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023

Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Water was disposed of. Utensils were sanitized. Proper utensil storing procedures reviewed with PIC. The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Bucket was placed nearby for ice scoop. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023
Non-food grade containers are being used to store ingredients. Establishment to purchase more food grade buckets. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

The refrigerator and prep unit was observed unable to maintain an adequate ambient temperature. All food was relocated to walk-in. Units to not be used until serviced/ adjusted. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the portable fan, inside prep unit and hood ventilation covers have an accumulation of dust, food residue, grease and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023
The walls and ceiling in the main kitchen was observed with accumulation of grease and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023
The 3-bay sink was observed leaking into a container. The 3-bay sink must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023
